response: Sarah owns a small retail shop that supports local factory workers by selling their handmade products. She decides to run a promotional week where she offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers. If a customer purchases a handcrafted table originally priced at $200 and a set of factory-made chairs originally priced at $150, how much does the customer pay for these items during the promotional week? Remember to apply the discount to each item separately. *** Reach the goal by calculating the discounted prices for each item and summing them. The customer pays $170 for the table and $127.50 for the chairs, totaling $297.50. The discount is applied to each item individually, ensuring the final price reflects the promotional offer. The total amount paid by the customer during the promotional week is $297.50. *** The promotional week offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers. The handcrafted table, originally priced at $200, is reduced by 15% to $170. The factory-made chairs, originally priced at $150, are reduced by 15% to $127.50. The customer pays $170 for the table and $127.50 for the chairs, totaling $297.50. The discount is applied to each item separately, ensuring the final price reflects the promotional offer. The total amount paid by the customer during the promotional week is $297.50. *** The promotional week offers a 15% discount on all items produced by the factory workers.
